solutions.title: Review the failing assertion
solutions.explanation: The test assertion failed: `{error_message}`. Check the test output for expected vs actual values. The test expected one value but got another — determine if the code or the test expectation is wrong.

solutions.title: Review recent code changes
solutions.explanation: Check `git diff HEAD~1` to see recent changes that may have altered the expected behavior. The test was passing before — determine what changed and whether the change is intentional.

solutions.title: Update test expectations if behavior is correct
solutions.explanation: If the new code behavior is intentionally correct (e.g. a feature change), update the test's expected values to match. Never change tests to pass without understanding why they failed.

detection.regex: TimeoutError|Timeout - Async callback was not invoked|timed out after (\d+)ms|jest.setTimeout|Test timeout of

solutions.title: Increase test timeout
solutions.explanation: The test exceeded the timeout limit of {timeout_ms}ms. For async integration tests, increase the timeout: `jest.setTimeout(30000)` (Jest) or `@pytest.mark.timeout(30)` (pytest). Don't increase for unit tests — slow unit tests indicate a problem.

solutions.title: Mock external dependencies
solutions.explanation: Tests shouldn't make real network calls or DB operations — these are slow and flaky. Mock HTTP calls with `msw` (Jest) or `responses` (pytest), mock DB with an in-memory store.

solutions.title: Check for infinite loops or deadlocks
solutions.explanation: The timeout may be caused by an infinite loop, unresolved Promise, or deadlock in the code under test. Add logs or use a debugger to trace where execution hangs.

detection.regex: Jest: .* coverage threshold for (.+) \((\d+)%\) not met|coverage for .* (\d+)% does not meet global threshold|ERROR: Coverage failure

solutions.title: Add tests for uncovered code
solutions.explanation: Run `npm test -- --coverage` (Jest) or `pytest --cov --cov-report=html` to see which files/lines are uncovered. Write targeted tests for the uncovered code paths — branches, edge cases, error handling.

solutions.title: Exclude non-critical files from coverage
solutions.explanation: Exclude generated files, migrations, or config files from coverage. In Jest: `coveragePathIgnorePatterns`. In pytest: add `omit = tests/*,migrations/*` to `.coveragerc`.

solutions.title: Temporarily lower threshold
solutions.explanation: If adding coverage is a gradual process, temporarily lower the threshold in `jest.config.ts` or `pyproject.toml`. Set a roadmap to increase it incrementally. Document the tech debt.

detection.regex: retries exhausted|test failed on retry|Flaky|non-deterministic|has been retried (\d+) times

solutions.title: Remove timing dependencies
solutions.explanation: Flaky tests often rely on `setTimeout`, `sleep()`, or wall-clock time. Replace with deterministic waits: `waitFor()` in Testing Library, `expect.poll()` in Playwright, or mock `Date.now()` / `datetime.now()`.

solutions.title: Properly mock external dependencies
solutions.explanation: Tests that make real network calls or depend on external services are inherently flaky. Use `msw` (mock service worker) for HTTP, `jest.spyOn()` for modules, or `unittest.mock` for Python.

solutions.title: Isolate test state properly
solutions.explanation: Tests that share state (global variables, DB records, file system) can interfere. Use `beforeEach`/`afterEach` to reset state, use a fresh DB transaction per test, or use test isolation frameworks.

solutions.title: Install test runner and dependencies
solutions.explanation: The test runner or a required module is missing. Run `npm install --save-dev jest` (or the appropriate test runner). For Python: `pip install pytest pytest-asyncio pytest-cov`.

solutions.title: Verify test configuration file
solutions.explanation: Check `jest.config.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, or `pytest.ini` for incorrect paths, transform rules, or moduleNameMapper entries that may prevent modules from being found.

solutions.title: Set NODE_ENV for test environment
solutions.explanation: Some configurations (dotenv, conditional imports) require `NODE_ENV=test` to load test-specific settings. Set it in CI: `env: NODE_ENV: test` in the workflow step.
